member.summary:
Builds a BotWorker — a self-contained, continuously running bot that pulls IWorkItems from a shared IWorkQueue and executes each one through AgentRuntime.
Deploy N BotWorkers across N machines pointing at the same IWorkQueue backend (swap InMemoryWorkQueue for a SQL / Service Bus / RabbitMQ implementation).
Call worker.Start() after building. Dispose with await worker.DisposeAsync().
member.param: Logical bot name shared across all instances of this bot type.
Workflow config. Call config.ApplyProfile() before passing, or let BuildBot do it. WorkflowType and AiParticipation are applied automatically.
Shared queue. Defaults to InMemoryWorkQueue (single-process only). For multi-machine, pass a shared broker-backed implementation.
Converts an IWorkItem into an IExecutionContext for the runtime. Defaults to an ExecutionContext with SessionId=WorkflowId, UserId=botId, Metadata populated from the item's Payload.
Max items processed in parallel per BotWorker instance.
Max retry attempts before dead-lettering a failed item.

member.name: T:Valaiorp.Runtime.Bot.BotWorker
member.summary:
Continuously running bot that pulls IWorkItems from a shared IWorkQueue and executes each one through AgentRuntime.
Deploy N BotWorkers across N machines all pointing at the same IWorkQueue backend (SqlWorkQueue for multi-machine, InMemoryWorkQueue for single-process).
Lifecycle: StartAsync() — registers a run, begins the poll loop (non-blocking) StopAsync() — drains in-flight work, ends the run, then stops DisposeAsync() — stop + dispose the underlying AgentRuntime
Retry / dead-letter (at queue level, outer layer above tool-level retry): On success → MarkCompletedAsync On failure + retry left → MarkFailedAsync (item re-queued automatically) On failure + max reached → MarkFailedAsync (item dead-lettered automatically)

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Runtime.Bot.BotWorker.StartAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Registers a queue run and starts the poll loop (non-blocking).

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Runtime.Bot.BotWorker.StopAsync
member.summary: Signals stop, drains in-flight items, and ends the queue run.

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Runtime.DependencyInjection.ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddSqlPersistence(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Func{System.Data.Common.DbConnection})
T:Valaiorp.Runtime.Logging.SqlExecutionLogger
M:Valaiorp.Runtime.Logging.SqlExecutionLogger.CreateSchemaAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates the execution_logs table if it does not exist. Call once at startup.

member.name: T:Valaiorp.Runtime.Logging.InlineSqlExecutionLogger
member.summary: Concrete SqlExecutionLogger backed by an inline connection factory. Used internally by services.AddSqlPersistence(factory).

member.name: T:Valaiorp.Runtime.Queue.InMemoryWorkQueue
member.summary: In-memory queue. Safe for single-process use (dev/test/single-bot). For multi-machine bots replace with SqlWorkQueue or a broker-backed implementation.

member.name: T:Valaiorp.Runtime.Queue.JsonlWorkQueue
member.summary:
JSONL file-backed queue. Suitable for single local bot, no external DB needed. Each work item is stored as one JSON line in the file. State is persisted on every status change so execution survives process restarts.
File path convention: {directory}/{queueId}.queue.jsonl — work items {directory}/{queueId}.runs.jsonl — run records

member.name: T:Valaiorp.Runtime.Queue.SqlWorkQueue
member.summary:
SQL-backed work queue. Works with any ADO.NET-compatible provider: SQL Server, PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, Oracle.
Subclass and override CreateConnection() to provide your connection. The SQL schema is standard ANSI SQL — see CreateSchemaAsync() for DDL.
Multi-machine safe: GetNextItemAsync uses a SELECT FOR UPDATE / UPDATE with optimistic locking so multiple bots never claim the same item.
Usage (SQL Server example): public sealed class MySqlQueue : SqlWorkQueue { protected override DbConnection CreateConnection() => new SqlConnection("Server=prod;Database=BotQueue;..."); }

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Runtime.Queue.SqlWorkQueue.CreateSchemaAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates the required tables if they do not exist. Call once at application startup.

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Runtime.AgentRuntime.EnrichContextWithRagAndMemoryAsync(Valaiorp.Core.Contracts.IExecutionContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Hydrates the context's PromptContext with RAG results and memory entries before handing it to the planner.
